codeReview.summary: The export client covers the core Linear pagination and file-writing flow, but the original team-scoping logic incorrectly retained users who only appeared in comments from other teams. That leaks unrelated-team users into scoped exports and makes the validation counts inaccurate for `involute export --team <key>`.

issues.description: `filterExportDataToTeamScope()` derives `users` from `allowedUserIds`, and that set is populated from comment authors for every comment file retained in `comments`. In the original implementation this allowed scoped exports to keep users who never belonged to the selected team's issues, leaking unrelated-team users into `users.json` and inflating validation counts. The bug is demonstrated by the original test expectation that an ENG-scoped export still included `usr-2`, whose only selected-team relationship came from another team's comment data.

summary: Reviewed the feature handoff, transcript skeleton, skill instructions, commit diff, and implementation files. Found one blocking correctness issue in the original team-scoping behavior: scoped exports could retain unrelated users via comment-author collection, so the feature did not fully satisfy the requirement that scoped export artifacts and counts contain no unrelated-team data.
